text:
Market today: Tech is under pressure. Microsoft's historic June selloff is dragging the whole sector — heavy AI spending is spooking investors who want returns, not promises. Memory crunch headlines add macro noise across chips. Your concentrated, margin-heavy book feels this directly.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm — down 17.6% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, act on this. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.2% and flagged TRIM — chip sentiment is shaky right now, size matters here. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 16.5% — decay plus leverage plus margin is a dangerous combo, keep it very tight to that $19 stop.

Watch: 1) Whether Microsoft's spending-driven selloff spreads to other AI-heavy names — PLTR and CRWD move in that current. 2) Memory/chip headlines around Micron — Micron momentum drives SMH, your one green chip position.

Bottom line: Your biggest risks are PLTR and AVGO — both flagged, both bleeding, both amplified by margin; those two deserve a decision today.
